test: End-to-end agent comparison: NDP-MCP+Bash vs CLIO pipeline
question: Find temperature measurements above 30 degrees Celsius from weather station datasets in the National Data Platform. Tell me how many rows match and give 3 example values (raw value and the station name if you can find it). Use the NDP-MCP tools to discover datasets, then fetch and parse the actual data.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.input: {'command': 'curl -sL "https://f3i-supercomputing.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/data/FresnoState_daily.csv" | head -n 5', 'description': 'Preview CIMIS Fresno State daily CSV'}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.input: {'command': 'curl -sL "https://hive.utah.edu/downloads/mg74qm138" | head -n 5', 'description': 'Preview Courthouse Mesa air temp CSV'}

mode_a_no_clio.answer:
All three datasets returned results. Here's the summary:

---

## Results: Temperature Measurements Above 30°C from NDP Weather Station Datasets

I searched the **National Data Platform** using the `search_datasets` MCP tool for weather station temperature data and found the **CIMIS (California Irrigation Management Information System)** weather station datasets with downloadable CSVs. Temperature units were inferred from the column headers (e.g., `Air Temp (C)` → Celsius), so no conversion was needed.

### Matching Row Counts

| Dataset | Station | Time Range | Column Used | Rows > 30°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CIMIS Hourly – Station #105 | Westlands | 2010–2025 | `Air Temp (C)` | **17,112** |
| CIMIS Hourly – Station #124 | Panoche | 2010–2025 | `Air Temp (C)` | **12,877** |
| CIMIS Daily – Station #80 | Fresno State | 2010–2014 | `Max Air Temp (C)` | **371** |

**Combined total across all 3 datasets: 30,360 rows** with temperatures above 30°C.

### 3 Example Values

| # | Station | Date/Time | Raw Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Westlands** (#105) | 9/28/2010, 11:00 PST | **32.7°C** | Celsius (from header `Air Temp (C)`) |
| 2 | **Panoche** (#124) | 9/28/2010, 13:0

mode_b_with_clio.answer:
Here are the results from searching the National Data Platform for temperature measurements above **30 °C**:

---

## Summary

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Datasets discovered on NDP** | 93 |
| **CSV resources downloaded & parsed** | 3 |
| **Total rows parsed** | 14,856 |
| **Rows matching (≥ 30 °C)** | **200** (≈ 1.35% of all rows) |

---

## 3 Example Matches

| # | Raw Value | Station | Canonical (Kelvin) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **30.5 °C** | Westlands (CIMIS #105) | 303.65 K |
| 2 | **32.7 °C** | Westlands (CIMIS #105) | 305.85 K |
| 3 | **34.5 °C** | Westlands (CIMIS #105) | 307.65 K |

---

## Breakdown by Resource

1. **CIMIS Station #105 – Westlands** — 88 matches out of 4,999 rows
2. **CIMIS Station #124 – Panoche** — 46 matches out of 5,000 rows
3. **CIMIS Station #125 – Arvin-Edison** — 66 matches out of 4,857 rows

All three datasets come from the **CIMIS Hourly Data** collection (California Irrigation Management Information System), covering hourly weather observations from 2010–2025.
